concerns.text: T050-T055 (Lag Handling & Data Alignment) are marked as pending `[ ]` but are semantically required producers for T020-T025 (US2 Implementation) and T053 (Integration). T020b/T020c explicitly require 'apply lag shift' logic which is defined in T050. T021/T051 require Neff calculation logic defined in T051. The current order places consumers (T020-T025) before their producers (T050-T055), violating the 'Producer before consumer' dependency rule. T053 is also pending, leaving the statistical pipeline incomplete.

T046a-T049c (Streaming & Memory Optimization) are marked `[X]` (complete), yet T013c (US1 Runner & Writer) depends on the streaming logic defined in T046b/T047b to handle the full dataset without OOM. If T046-T049 are truly complete, T013c should be able to execute. However, the 'Analyze Report' indicates T013c output (`synced.csv`) is missing, suggesting the streaming implementation is either incomplete or the tasks are incorrectly marked as done. This creates a semantic contradiction: a 'complete' producer (T046-T049) failing to enable its consumer (T013c).

T003d, T003e, T003g (Fixture Generation) are marked `[X]` but T010, T019, T028 (Integration Tests) depend on these fixtures. The 'Analyze Report' states these fixtures are missing. If the tasks are marked complete, the artifacts must exist. This indicates a false-positive completion status for the producers (T003d-g), breaking the dependency chain for the consumers (T010, T019, T028).

T048a/T048b (Memmap/Chunked Autocorrelation) are marked `[X]` in Phase 2.6, but T051 (Neff calculation logic) is marked `[ ]` in Phase N+2. T021 (US2 Implementation) depends on Neff logic. The split of Neff logic between a 'complete' chunked loader (T048) and a 'pending' calculation logic (T051) creates a fragmented producer. T021 cannot be implemented until T051 is complete, yet T021 is listed in Phase 4 (implied to be after Phase 2.6). The ordering implies T021 can proceed before T051, which is impossible.

T043a/T043b (Data Source Verification) are marked `[X]` but the Plan.md Constitution Check explicitly states Principle II (Verified Accuracy) is FAIL due to missing NOAA URL. This contradiction suggests the tasks are marked complete without actually resolving the dependency (the URL definition). While not a strict ordering violation, it implies the 'producer' (T043a) has not actually produced the required artifact (verified URL) for the consumer (T043b/T011) to use reliably.
